title: Mamendin
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:KidietOlgo-TEA.jpg]] |name=Mamendin |region=*[[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *[[Northern Dependencies/Legends|Northern Dependencies]] |sector=[[Farrfin sector/Legends|Farrfin sector]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Mamendin system/Legends|Mamendin system]] |system=[[Mamendin system/Legends|Mamendin system]] |coordinates=K-9 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons= |routes= |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class=Terrestrial{{CSWECite|III|291|Valadon}} |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity= |terrain= |water= |interest= |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies= |socialgroup= |language= |government= |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports= |exports= |affiliation=[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] }} {{Quote|They are called the Slams.''"
"''Species?''"
"''Humanoid. From Mamendin, in the Core. They started there with con jobs, ID thefts, things like that.|Tyro Caladian and Obi-Wan Kenobi|''[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard]]''}} '''Mamendin''' was a [[Planet/Legends|world]] situated in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. It [[Colonist/Legends|colonized]] the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Malari system/Legends|Malari system]], and, by the time of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], the [[Malarian Alliance]] bound it to that [[Star system/Legends|system]] as well as, ultimately, around forty others. By [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]], Mamendin was where the [[Slams]] gang of criminals [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed from]]. [[22 BBY/Legends|Two years later]], members of the Malarian Alliance were considering withdrawing from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. A [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] plot to ensure that outcome did not succeed, and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] later declared the Malarian Alliance to be null and void. ==Description== Mamendin was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Mamendin system/Legends|Mamendin system]] of the [[Farrfin sector/Legends|Farrfin sector]], a part of the [[Northern Dependencies/Legends|Northern Dependencies]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. It was situated on a [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace route]] that connected it to the [[Farrfin system/Legends|Farrfin]] and [[Kidiet Olgo system/Legends|Kidiet Olgo systems]]. ==History== {{Quote|If we can get the Ansionians to commit to pulling out of the Republic, no one will really care. But because of their alliances, their withdrawal should be enough to sway their already vacillating partners in both the Malarian Alliance and the Keitumite Treaty to follow.|[[Presidente/Legends|Presidente]] [[Shu Mai/Legends|Shu Mai]]|''[[The Approaching Storm]]''}} [[File:Ansion-TEA.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Malarian Alliance linked Mamendin to Ansion (pictured).]] [[Colonist/Legends|Settlers]] from Mamendin colonized the [[Malari system/Legends|Malari system]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. By [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' establishes that the [[Malari system/Legends|Malari system]] was a part of the [[Malarian Alliance]] by the time of the events of ''[[The Approaching Storm]]'', which ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. the Malari [[Star system/Legends|system]] was linked to Mamendin, a member world of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], both commercially and militarily via the [[Malarian Alliance]], a treaty{{CSWECite|II|279|Malarian Alliance}} that had been established at some point by approximately [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]].''[[The Clone Wars: Secret Missions 4: Guardians of the Chiss Key]]'' establishes that the [[Malarian Alliance]] was in effect eleven [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], which corresponds to approximately [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. The Malarian Alliance placed Mamendin in a web of mutual affiliations with three Mid Rim [[Star/Legends|star]] systems: the Malari system as well as the [[Ansion system/Legends|Ansion]] and [[Dbari system/Legends|Dbari systems]]. In addition, Mamendin, its partner systems, and members of the [[Keitumite Mutual Military Treaty]]—also affiliated with the Malari system—all had treaties of their own with other worlds, which led to the seemingly insignificant planet [[Ansion/Legends|Ansion]] lying at the center of a network of approximately forty [[Politics/Legends|politically]] connected systems. [[File:Kenobi-TheChangingOfTheGuardJapanese.jpg|thumb|left|400px|Tyro Caladian let Obi-Wan Kenobi (pictured) know about the Slams' origin on Mamendin.]] By [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard]]'' to [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]]. Mamendin was the point of origin of the [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Slams]] gang, who began their criminal career there with crimes such as [[Con artist/Legends|con]] jobs and identity thefts before moving off-world to the rest of the Core Worlds. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the Republic{{CSWECite|I|311|Galactic Senate}} [[Senatorial aide]] [[Tyro Caladian]] relayed that fact to the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] while letting him know about the Slams' recent escape from [[Greylands Security Complex|prison]]. During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], signatory systems of both the Malarian Alliance and the Keitumite Mutual Military Treaty were individually considering leaving the Republic. The [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] intended to facilitate the secession of Ansion from the Republic, which was predicted by the Separatists to cause a cascade of secession by all the systems linked to the planet via its treaties, as well. However, a [[Border dispute on Ansion/Legends|mission to Ansion]] undertaken in 22 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]] by members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] on behalf of the Republic resulted in that world and its partners remaining loyal to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government, foiling the Separatist plans. At some point following the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the newly appointed Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of the Mid Rim's [[Churnis sector/Legends|Churnis sector]] declared all existing treaties and alliances involving Ansion to be null and void. ==Behind the scenes== Mamendin was first mentioned in [[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard|''The Changing of the Guard'']], [[Jude Watson]]'s [[2004]] eighth installment of the ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Quest]]'' young-reader novel series. The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed it in grid square K-9. ==Appearances== {{Indexpage}} {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Mamendin}} *''[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard]]'' {{1stm}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|III||Valadon}} *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Farrfin sector locations]] [[Category:Galactic Republic planets]] [[Category:Planets in the Core Worlds]] [[Category:Planets in the Northern Dependencies]] [[Category:Terrestrial planets]]
